REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS THE ANDREWS HOTEL AND PASCALE SCHMIDT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 30 DAYS FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS, IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF CAUSE(S) OF ACTION SUBJECT TO THE DEMURRER. THE COURT MAKES NO DETERMINATIONS AS TO THE SUFFICIENCY OF PROPOSED AMENDMENTS PRESENTED IN NOVEMBER 1, 2022 DECLARATION. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: W. BY NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 1, 2022 PLAINTIFF TO FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION INCLUDING A COPY OF THE PROPOSED AMENDMENTS TO THE COMPLAINT INCLUDING FACTS SUPPORTING PLAINTIFF'S CONCLUSORY ALLEGATIONS, E.G. AS TO PARAGRAPH 24 FACTS SUPPORTING ALLEGATIONS THAT D WAS "AWARE OF BEDBUG INFESTATION IN THE HOTEL AND, SPECIFICALLY, PLAINTIFF'S ROOM.;" AS TO PARAGRAPH 18 DATES OF ON-LINE REVIEWS AND/OR LINKS TO SPECIFIC PAGES, WHETHER D RESPONDED TO ANY POSTINGS ON THE REFERENCED SITES, ETC; WHETHER DS WERE NOTIFIED IN ANY OTHER MANNER OF THE BED BUG PRESENCE AND DATES OF SUCH NOTIFICATIONS PRIOR TO PLAINTIFF'S STAY; AS TO PARAGRAPH 38 FACTS SUPPORTING THE CONCLUSION THAT "DEFENDANTS DELIBERATELY CHOSE TO NOT ERADICATE THE BEDBUG INFESTATION." JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Off Calendar Oct-20-2022 Continued To Nov-10-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANTS THE ANDREWS HOTEL AND PASCALE SCHMIDT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to November 10, 2022. By no later than November 1, 2022 Plaintiff to file a supplemental declaration including a copy of the proposed amendments to the complaint including facts supporting Plaintiff's conclusory allegations, e.g. as to paragraph 24 facts supporting allegations that D was "aware of bedbug infestation in the hotel and, specifically, Plaintiff's room.;" as to paragraph 18 dates of on-line reviews and/or links to specific pages, whether D responded to any postings on the referenced sites, etc; whether Ds were notified in any other manner of the bed bug presence and dates of such notifications prior to Plaintiff's stay; as to paragraph 38 facts supporting the conclusion that "defendants deliberately chose to not eradicate the bedbug infestation."

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
